original_image_tags: A typewritten document on aged paper. A faint blue handwritten number "54" and "20" are visible in the left margin. A blue pen mark crosses out the "S" in "STRINGFIELD" in the first paragraph. A small red handwritten note, possibly "Ok. J.D.", is in the right margin next to the second paragraph.

elements.description: Two red rubber stamp impressions near the bottom: 'COPIES DESTROYED 270 NOV 19 1964' and '62-83894-350'. A pink/red rectangular stamp with text 'NO CLOSURE' partially visible. Blue ink handwritten annotations at the bottom including 'INTERNAL SECURITY - 1 -' and a signature/initials. A red ink handwritten notation 'obto' in the right margin.

judge.reason: The descriptions are about entirely different visual content; Description A describes a typewritten document with handwritten margin notes, while Description B describes rubber stamps and handwritten annotations at the bottom of a page.
